questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The angle between the straight lines, whose direction cosines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>m</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi></math> are given by the equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>l</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mi>l</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>lm</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, is:
questions.solution:
Given equations of direction cosies

2ℓ+2m-n=0 ...............i
mn+nℓ+ℓm=0..............ii
ℓm+n(ℓ+m)=0

From equation i

n=2ℓ+m
ℓm+2(ℓ+m)2=0
2ℓ2+2m2+5ℓm=0

Dividing by m2 on both sides
2ℓm2+2+5ℓm=0
Let ℓm=t
2t2+5t+2=0

2t2+4t+t+2=0

t+22t+1=0
t=-2,-12

Case 1

ℓm=−12

m=−2ℓ, n=-2ℓ

(ℓ,−2ℓ,−2ℓ)⇒(1,−2,−2)

Case 2

ℓm=-2

ℓ=-2m, n=-2m

-2m,m,-2m⇒-2,1,-2

cosθ=1×-2+-2×1+-2×-212+-22+-22-22+12+-22

cosθ=-2-2+49=0

θ=π2

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If the length of the perpendicular drawn from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></math> units and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is the image of the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> in this line, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></munderover><msub><mi>α</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let a point on the line x+12=y-33=z-1-1=λ be

M2λ-1,3λ+3,-λ+1

and the DR's of the line are 2,3,-1

The DR's of line joining the points 2λ-1,3λ+3,-λ+1 and a,4,2 will be

2λ-1-a,3λ-1,-λ-1

Now its perpendicular to the given line, so2λ-1-a2+3λ-13+-λ-1-1=0

⇒4λ-2-2a+9λ-3+λ+1=0

⇒14λ-4-2a=0

⇒7λ-2-a=0⇒7λ-2=a

and,

2λ-1-a2+3λ-12+λ+12=262

⇒5λ-12+3λ-12+λ+12=24

⇒35λ2-14λ-21=0

⇒λ-135λ+21=0

For, λ=1; a=5

i.e. M≡1,6,0

As Qα1,α2,α3 is the image of point P

α1+52=1;α2+42=6;α3+22=0

⇒α1=-3;α2=8;α3=-2

Hence, a+α1+α2+α3=8

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Let the position vectors of two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>, respectively. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> be two points such that the direction ratios of lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mi>S</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, respectively. Let lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mi>S</mi></math> intersect at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math>. If the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mrow><mi>T</mi><mi>A</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is perpendicular to both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mrow><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mrow><mi>Q</mi><mi>S</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and the length of vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mrow><mi>T</mi><mi>A</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> units, then the modulus of a position vector of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is :
questions.solution:
P3,-1,2

Q1,2,-4

PR→ ‖ 4i^-j^+2k^

QS→ ‖ -2i^+j^-2k^

Dr's of normal to the plane containing P,T and Q will be proportional to : 

i^j^k^4-12-21-2=4j^+2k^

∴ ℓ0=m4=n2

For point, T : PT→=x-34=y+1-1=z-22=λ

QT→=x-1-2=y-21=z+4-2=μ

T≡4λ+3,-λ-1,2λ+2

Q≡2μ+1,μ+2,-2μ-4

4λ+3=-2μ+1⇒2λ+μ=-1

λ+μ=-3⇒λ=2

and μ=-5, λ+μ=-3⇒λ=2

So point T:11,-3,6

OA→=11i^-3j^+6k^±2j^+k^55

OA→=11i^-3j^+6k^±2j^+k^

OA→=11i^-j^+7k^ or 9i^-5j^+5k^

OA→=121+1+49=171 or 81+25+25=131

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> passes through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and is perpendicular to the plane <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></math>. Then the shortest distance between the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is:
questions.solution:
The line l1 passes through the point (2, 6, 2) and is perpendicular to the line 2x+y-2z=10 then,

l1: x-22=y-61=z-2-2 

Let the shortest distance between l1 and the line x+12=y+4-3=z2 is S.D.

S.D. =AB→·MN→MN→

Here, AB→=3i^+10j^+2k^

MN→=i^j^k^21-22-32=-4i^-8j^-8k^

⇒MN→=16+64+64=12

AB→·MN→=-12-80-16=-108

So, shortest distance (S.D.) 

=-10812=-9=9 units

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For real numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> if the point of intersection of the straight lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></mrow><mn>1</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mi>β</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> lies on the plane <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to :
questions.solution:
Given, x-α1=y-12=z-13 and x-4β=y-63=z-73 lies on the plane x+2y-z=8.

Let x-α1=y-12=z-13=∅

and x-4β=y-63=z-73=q

Any point on the first line can be considered as ϕ+α, 2ϕ+1, 3ϕ+1

and a point on the second line can be qβ+4,3q+6,3q+7.

For intersection of the lines ϕ+α=qβ+4       ...1

2ϕ+1=3q+6        ...2

3ϕ+1=3q+7       ...3

For 2 & 3 ϕ=1,q=-1

So, from 1 α+β=3

Now, point of intersection is α+1,3,4

It lies on the given plane, therefore, α+1+2×3-4=8⇒α=5

 ⇒β=-2 from α+β=3

Hence, α-β=5--2=7

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>·</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>·</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>|</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><msup><mo>|</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to :

questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></math> be a vector which makes equal angles with the coordinate axes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>O</mi><mi>X</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>O</mi><mi>Y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>O</mi><mi>Z</mi></math>. Also, let the projection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> on the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> . Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be a vector obtained by rotating <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis are coplanar, then projection of a vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given a→=a1i^+a2j^+a3k^

Since the vector makes equal angles with the coordinate axes OX,OY and OZ, so

a→=λ13i^+13j^+13k^=λ3i^+j^+k^

Now projection of a→on b→=7

⇒λ3i^+j^+k^·3i^+4j^5=7

⇒λ=53

So a→=5i^^+j^+k^

Let b→=5αi^+j^+k^+βi^

Since both vectors are perpendicular a→·b→=0

⇒25α3+5β=0

⇒15α+β=0⇒β=-15α

i.e. b→=5α-2i^+j^+k^

b→=53

b→=±52-2i^+j^+k^

Projection of b→ on 3i^+4j^ is

b→·3i^+4j^5=±52-6+45=±2

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> be two vectors, such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>. Then the projection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let a→=αi^+j^+βk^,b→=3i^-5j^+4k^

Now finding a→×b→=-i^+9j^+12k^

=i^j^k^α1β3-54

⇒ 4+5βi^+3β-4αj^+-5α-3k^

=-i^+9j^+12k^

Now on comparing side we get,

 4+5β=-1,3β-4α=9,-5α-3=12

β=-1,  α=-3

So, a→=-3i^+j^-k^ & b→=3i^-5j^+4k^

So, a→+b→=-4j^+3k^

Now finding a→2=11 & b→2=50

And a→·b→=-9+-5-4=-18

Now projection of b→-2a→ on a→+b→ will be 

=b→-2a→·a→+b→a→+b→

=b→2-2a→2-a→·b→a→+b→=50-22--185=465

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>. Then the number of vectors <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Given, a→=i+j-k and c→=2i-3j+2k

b→×c→=a→ and b→∈1,2……10

∵ b→×c→=a→

⇒ a→ is perpendicular to b→ as well as a→ is perpendicular to c→ , so a→·c→=0 as they are perpendicular.

Now checking a→·c→=2-3-2=-3≠0

This b→×c→=a→ is not possible.

So, number of vectors b→=0

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be the vectors along the diagonal of a parallelogram having area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></math>. Let the angle between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be acute. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>.</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover accent="true"><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mfenced><mrow><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mover accent="true"><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>, then an angle between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is

questions.q: Let the vectors <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>c</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mi>t</mi><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi><mover><mi>j</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>t</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> be such that for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mn>0</mn><mo>→</mo></mover></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>⇒</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Then, the set of all values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Given the vectors a→=1+ti^+1-tj^+k^, b→=1-ti^+1+tj^+2k^ and c→=ti^-tj^+k^, t∈R be such that for α,β,γ∈R, αa→+βb→+γc→=0→ ⇒α=β=γ=0. Then, the set of all values of t is

By its given condition :a→,b→,c→are linearly independent vectors or they are non-coplanar

We know that when vectors are non-coplanar then, a→b→c→≠0     ...1

Now, a→b→c→

=1+t1-t11-t1+t2t-t1

C2→C1+C2

=1+t211-t22t01

=21+t111-t12t01

=21+t-1-t+t

=23t=6t

Now from equation 1 we get, a→b→c→≠0⇒t≠0

questions.q:
Let A, B, C be three points whose position vectors respectively are:

a→=i^+4j^+3k^

b→=2i^+αj^+4k^, α∈R

c→=3i^-2j^+5k^

If α is the smallest positive integer for which a→, b→, c→ are non-collinear, then the length of the median, △ABC, through A is:
questions.solution:
Let us assume that vectors are collinear so, AB→∥AC→ if 12=α-4-6=12⇒α=1 

Now, for a→, b→, c→ to be non-collinear smallest positive integer will be α=2

So, mid-point of BC=M52, 0, 92

Now, AM=94+16+94=822

questions.q: The vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> is rotated through a right angle, passing through the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math>-axis in its way and the resulting vector is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>. Then the projection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> is
questions.solution:
Given,

The vector a→=-i^+2j^+k^ is rotated through a right angle, passing through the y-axis in its way and the resulting vector is b→

Now taking, b→=λa→×a→×j^ {note here we have taken cross vector with j^ as it is rotated along y-axis}

⇒b→=λ-i^+2j^+k^×-i^+2j^+k^×j^

⇒b→=λ-i^+2j^+k^×-i^-k^

⇒b→=λi^j^k^-121-10-1

⇒b→=λ-2i^-2j^+2k^

Also magnitude of both vector will be equal,

So,b→=a→⇒6=12 λ

⇒λ=±12

(Now λ=12 rejected ∵  b→ makes acute angle with y axis )

So, b→=-2-i^-j^+k^

Now projection of  3a→+2b→ on c→ is given by,

3a→+2b·c→c→=32

questions.solution:
Given:

v→×w→=u→+λv→   ....1

Taking dot product with w→, we get

w→·v→×w→=w→·u→+λw→·v→

⇒u→·w→+λw→·v→=0

⇒u→·w→=-2λ   ....2

Taking dot product with v→ in 1, we get

v→·v→×w→=v→·u→+λv→·v→

⇒2-1+2+λ6+1+1=0

⇒λ=-12

So, by 2, we get

u→·w→=-2λ=1
